Democrats want Jeff Sessions to investigate. Seven transgender women have been killed this year. Democrats want Jeff Sessions to investigate. The deaths of the seven transgender women this year have occurred under tragic and often violent circumstances, prompting some Democrats to call on then-Attorney General Jeff Sessions to open a federal investigation into whether these cases represent a broader pattern of hate crimes targeting transgender individuals.

China plans a new moon probe in response to possible return there by U.S. China plans a new moon probe in response to possible return there by U.S. China plans a new moon probe in response to possible return there by U.S. China's new planned moon probe is part of the Chang'e lunar exploration program, specifically the Chang'e-6 mission. The main objective of Chang'e-6 is to land on the far side of the moon and collect lunar samples—the first mission to do so from this part of the moon. The probe will use a lander, an ascender, an orbiter, and a return capsule. Its scientific goals include returning at least 2 kilograms of lunar soil and rock samples to Earth for extensive analysis. These samples will provide insights into the moon's composition, geology, and history.

You’re more likely to die from air pollution in India than China, study says You’re more likely to die from air pollution in India than China, study says You’re more likely to die from air pollution in India than China, study says The main sources of air pollution in India and China share some similarities but also exhibit key differences. In India, major sources include road vehicle emissions, the burning of biomass, industrial emissions, and dust from construction activities. In China, industrial emissions, coal combustion for power and heating, and vehicle emissions are primary contributors. Despite both countries facing severe air pollution issues, recent studies indicate that air pollution-related mortality rates have become higher in India than in China, largely due to differences in pollution control measures and the implementation of stricter regulations in China over the past decade.

How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways Amphetamine use can affect waterways because, after being consumed, these substances are excreted in urine and can enter wastewater systems. Traditional wastewater treatment plants are often not fully effective at removing amphetamines, so these drugs can end up in rivers, lakes, and other aquatic environments. Studies have shown that even trace amounts of amphetamines in waterways can impact aquatic organisms, such as fish and invertebrates, potentially altering their behavior, reproduction, and ecosystem balance.

How China’s fishermen are fighting a covert war in the South China Sea How China’s fishermen are fighting a covert war in the South China Sea How China’s fishermen are fighting a covert war in the South China Sea China's fishermen in the South China Sea employ several tactics that serve both economic and strategic purposes. Some common tactics include fishing in contested waters to assert China's territorial claims and operating in coordination with China's maritime militia to reinforce the country's presence in disputed areas. These activities are often considered part of a broader campaign to strengthen China's control over the region, sometimes referred to as a 'covert war' due to the blend of civilian and military objectives.

Websites where children are prostituted are immune from prosecution. But why? Websites where children are prostituted are immune from prosecution. But why? Websites where children are prostituted are immune from prosecution. But why? Historically, websites that host user-generated content have been afforded broad legal protections under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act (CDA) of 1996. Section 230 stipulates that providers or users of an 'interactive computer service' are not considered the publisher or speaker of information posted by third parties. This legal protection enabled websites to avoid liability for much of the content their users posted, including, regrettably, content related to child prostitution or other illegal activities, as long as the site itself did not create or directly contribute to the illegal posts. Aging-in-place options most popular with baby boomers Aging-in-place options most popular with baby boomers Aging-in-place options most popular with baby boomers Aging-in-place options most popular with baby boomers generally center around maintaining independence, comfort, and safety while remaining in one's own home as long as possible. Popular options include modifying existing homes to make them safer and more accessible, utilizing in-home care services, and leveraging technology such as medical alert systems and smart home devices. Some baby boomers also choose to move to active adult communities, co-housing arrangements, or multigenerational homes with family members. However, the overwhelming preference is to stay in familiar surroundings, adapting the home as needed rather than moving to traditional assisted living or nursing facilities.

Sun erupts to mark another Bastille Day, aurora possible in New England Sunday night Sun erupts to mark another Bastille Day, aurora possible in New England Sunday night Sun erupts to mark another Bastille Day, aurora possible in New England Sunday night The connection between Bastille Day and solar eruptions refers to the major solar storm that occurred on July 14, 2000—Bastille Day in France. This event is known as the Bastille Day solar storm. Similar solar eruptions, like the recent one, can produce auroras that may be visible farther south than usual, including places like New England.

Teen birthrate hits all-time low, led by 50 percent decline among Hispanics and blacks Teen birthrate hits all-time low, led by 50 percent decline among Hispanics and blacks Teen birthrate hits all-time low, led by 50 percent decline among Hispanics and blacks Several factors have contributed to the decline in teen birthrates among Hispanics and blacks in the United States. Key contributors include increased access to and use of contraception, better sex education, broader availability of health services, and community programs aimed at reducing teen pregnancy. These efforts, combined with shifting social attitudes and greater educational and economic opportunities for young people, have all played significant roles in decreasing teen birthrates among these groups.

Their murders highlight the growing risks and dangers faced by journalists in Mexico, where criminal organizations and impunity contribute to a hostile environment for the press.

Chesapeake Bay earns ‘C’ for overall health; blue crabs, rockfish, anchovies are thriving Chesapeake Bay earns ‘C’ for overall health; blue crabs, rockfish, anchovies are thriving Chesapeake Bay earns ‘C’ for overall health; blue crabs, rockfish, anchovies are thriving The Chesapeake Bay received a 'C' grade for overall health due to ongoing challenges like nutrient and sediment pollution, often from agriculture, urban runoff, and wastewater. These factors contribute to problems such as algal blooms, poor water clarity, and loss of underwater grasses, affecting habitats for fish and wildlife. However, despite these issues, populations of blue crabs, rockfish, and anchovies are reported to be thriving. Restoration and recovery efforts are ongoing, but improvements have been uneven throughout the watershed.

Why the FDA’s new e-cigarette regulations are a gift to Big Tobacco (and could actually harm public health) Why the FDA’s new e-cigarette regulations are a gift to Big Tobacco (and could actually harm public health) Why the FDA’s new e-cigarette regulations are a gift to Big Tobacco (and could actually harm public health) The FDA’s new e-cigarette regulations require companies to submit expensive and complex premarket tobacco product applications (PMTAs) for each product and flavor. This process can be prohibitively costly for small and independent e-cigarette companies, but established tobacco companies with more resources can more easily manage these requirements. As a result, the regulations could limit competition, potentially giving Big Tobacco a larger share of the e-cigarette market, and may reduce the availability of less harmful alternatives for smokers—potentially harming public health.

Social Security trust fund will be empty in less than 20 years Social Security trust fund will be empty in less than 20 years Social Security trust fund will be empty in less than 20 years Several key factors are causing the Social Security trust fund to run low on money. First, demographic changes—such as the aging population and lower birth rates—mean there are fewer workers paying into the system for each retiree drawing benefits. Second, people are living longer and collecting benefits for more years. Without changes to revenue or benefit formulas, projections from the Social Security Trustees indicate the trust fund could be depleted within the next two decades.

New data shows a deadly measles complication is more common than thought New data shows a deadly measles complication is more common than thought New data shows a deadly measles complication is more common than thought The deadly measles complication recently highlighted in new data is known as subacute sclerosing panencephalitis (SSPE), a rare but fatal brain disorder that can develop years after a person has had measles. Recent studies suggest that SSPE may be more common than previously thought, especially among children who contract measles at a very young age.

Did a drone hit a British Airways plane? Did a drone hit a British Airways plane? Did a drone hit a British Airways plane? The alleged incident involving a British Airways plane and a drone occurred on April 17, 2016. The aircraft, an Airbus A320, was approaching Heathrow Airport when the pilot reported that he believed the plane had been hit by a drone. However, after investigation, the UK government concluded that it was likely the object was not a drone. No damage was found to the plane and there were no injuries reported.

Conservatives are more likely to believe that vaccines cause autism Conservatives are more likely to believe that vaccines cause autism Conservatives are more likely to believe that vaccines cause autism Research indicates that, in the United States, conservatives are not necessarily more likely than liberals to believe that vaccines cause autism. Large-scale surveys generally show that belief in the discredited link between vaccines and autism does not significantly differ by political ideology, though both groups may have some individuals who hold misinformed views. Vaccine hesitancy can be influenced by other factors such as trust in government, education level, and exposure to misinformation.

Baseball’s minor leaguers pursue their dreams below the poverty line Baseball’s minor leaguers pursue their dreams below the poverty line Baseball’s minor leaguers pursue their dreams below the poverty line The typical salaries and working conditions for minor league baseball players have historically been quite modest compared to those in Major League Baseball. As of 2023, many minor leaguers earn salaries that place them below the poverty line, with pay often ranging from a few thousand to a few tens of thousands of dollars per season, depending on the level. This financial hardship affects their ability to pursue their professional baseball dreams and has led to calls for increased wages and improved conditions.

Plant-based diets are often lower in calories and fat, and higher in fiber, which can help you feel fuller for longer and reduce overall calorie intake. Additionally, people who cut out meat often consume more fruits, vegetables, and whole grains, which are nutrient-dense and support weight loss.
